assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared so that a recognised intragroup receivable/payable exists.
That receivable/payable is a monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset or liability to be designated in a fair value hedge, and paragraph 6.3.6 specifically permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also permits a recognised asset or liability to be hedged for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, making this hedge model relevant to a foreign-currency intragroup monetary receivable.
